quiz.title: Unit 8 Quiz 11, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Mole Fractions, Molarity from Percent, and Gas Law Applications
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Mole Fraction Calculations, Molarity from Percent by Mass and Density, Gas Volume at STP, Atomic Mass from Gas Data, Triatomic and Tetraatomic Gases

questions.question: A mixture contains 3 g of $\text{H}_2$ and $\text{O}_2$ with mole fraction of $\text{H}_2 = 0.2$. Find mole fraction of $\text{O}_2$.
questions.explanation:
The sum of mole fractions in a mixture equals 1.

Mole fraction of O₂ = 1 - mole fraction of H₂ = 1 - 0.2 = 0.8

questions.question: What is the molarity of a 50% (by mass) $\text{KOH}$ solution if density is 1.5 g/mL?
questions.explanation:
Step 1: 50% by mass means 50 g KOH in 100 g solution.

Step 2: Volume of 100 g solution = 100/1.5 = 66.67 mL = 0.06667 L

Step 3: Molar mass KOH = 56.11 g/mol
Moles KOH = 50/56.11 = 0.891 mol

Step 4: Molarity = 0.891/0.06667 = 13.36 M ≈ 13.4 M

questions.question: 672 mL of a triatomic gas weighs 3 g at 273 K and 1 atm. Determine molar mass of the gas.
questions.explanation:
Step 1: At STP, 22.4 L = 1 mol
Moles = 0.672 L / 22.4 L/mol = 0.03 mol

Step 2: Molar mass = mass/moles = 3/0.03 = 100 g/mol

questions.question: A mixture contains 4 g of $\text{CH}_4$ and $\text{CO}$ with mole fraction of $\text{CH}_4 = 0.5$. Find mole fraction of $\text{CO}$.
questions.explanation:
The sum of mole fractions in a mixture equals 1.

Mole fraction of CO = 1 - mole fraction of CH₄ = 1 - 0.5 = 0.5

questions.question: Calculate the molarity of a 15% (by mass) $\text{C}_2\text{H}_5\text{OH}$ solution if density is 0.98 g/mL.
questions.explanation:
Step 1: 15% by mass means 15 g ethanol in 100 g solution.

Step 2: Volume of 100 g solution = 100/0.98 = 102.04 mL = 0.10204 L

Step 3: Molar mass ethanol = 46.07 g/mol
Moles ethanol = 15/46.07 = 0.3256 mol

Step 4: Molarity = 0.3256/0.10204 = 3.19 M

questions.question: 224 mL of a tetraatomic gas weighs 1.5 g at 273 K and 1 atm. Find molar mass of the gas.
questions.explanation:
Step 1: At STP, 22.4 L = 1 mol
Moles = 0.224 L / 22.4 L/mol = 0.01 mol

Step 2: Molar mass = mass/moles = 1.5/0.01 = 150 g/mol

questions.question: A mixture has 1 g of $\text{H}_2$ and $\text{He}$ with mole fraction of $\text{H}_2 = 0.6$. Find mole fraction of $\text{He}$.
questions.explanation:
The sum of mole fractions in a mixture equals 1.

Mole fraction of He = 1 - mole fraction of H₂ = 1 - 0.6 = 0.4

questions.question: Determine the molarity of a 35% (by mass) NaOH solution if density is 1.4 g/mL.
questions.explanation:
Step 1: 35% by mass means 35 g NaOH in 100 g solution.

Step 2: Volume of 100 g solution = 100/1.4 = 71.43 mL = 0.07143 L

Step 3: Molar mass NaOH = 40.00 g/mol
Moles NaOH = 35/40.00 = 0.875 mol

Step 4: Molarity = 0.875/0.07143 = 12.25 M ≈ 12.3 M

questions.question: 448 mL of a triatomic gas weighs 2 g at 273 K and 1 atm. Find molar mass of the gas.
questions.explanation:
Step 1: At STP, 22.4 L = 1 mol
Moles = 0.448 L / 22.4 L/mol = 0.02 mol

Step 2: Molar mass = mass/moles = 2/0.02 = 100 g/mol

questions.question: A mixture contains 6 g of $\text{N}_2$ and $\text{O}_2$ with mole fraction of $\text{O}_2 = 0.3$. Find mole fraction of $\text{N}_2$.
questions.explanation:
The sum of mole fractions in a mixture equals 1.

Mole fraction of N₂ = 1 - mole fraction of O₂ = 1 - 0.3 = 0.7

questions.question: Calculate the molarity of a 12% (by mass) $\text{Na}_2\text{CO}_3$ solution if density is 1.1 g/mL.
questions.explanation:
Step 1: 12% by mass means 12 g Na₂CO₃ in 100 g solution.

Step 2: Volume of 100 g solution = 100/1.1 = 90.91 mL = 0.09091 L

Step 3: Molar mass Na₂CO₃ = 105.99 g/mol
Moles Na₂CO₃ = 12/105.99 = 0.1132 mol

Step 4: Molarity = 0.1132/0.09091 = 1.245 M ≈ 1.25 M

questions.question: 336 mL of a diatomic gas weighs 1.2 g at 273 K and 1 atm. Determine mass of one atom of the gas.
questions.explanation:
Step 1: At STP, 22.4 L = 1 mol
Moles = 0.336 L / 22.4 L/mol = 0.015 mol

Step 2: Molar mass = 1.2/0.015 = 80 g/mol

Step 3: Diatomic gas → atomic mass = 80/2 = 40 g/mol

Step 4: Mass of one atom = 40 / $6.022 \times 10^{23}$ = $6.64 \times 10^{-23}$ g

questions.question: A mixture contains 5 g of $\text{CO}$ and $\text{CO}_2$ with mole fraction of $\text{CO} = 0.6$. Find mole fraction of $\text{CO}_2$.
questions.explanation:
The sum of mole fractions in a mixture equals 1.

Mole fraction of CO₂ = 1 - mole fraction of CO = 1 - 0.6 = 0.4

questions.question: Determine the molarity of a 22% (by mass) $\text{HCl}$ solution if density is 1.1 g/mL.
questions.explanation:
Step 1: 22% by mass means 22 g HCl in 100 g solution.

Step 2: Volume of 100 g solution = 100/1.1 = 90.91 mL = 0.09091 L

Step 3: Molar mass HCl = 36.46 g/mol
Moles HCl = 22/36.46 = 0.6034 mol

Step 4: Molarity = 0.6034/0.09091 = 6.64 M

questions.question: 224 mL of a triatomic gas weighs 1 g at 273 K and 1 atm. Find the molar mass of the gas.
questions.explanation:
Step 1: At STP, 22.4 L = 1 mol
Moles = 0.224 L / 22.4 L/mol = 0.01 mol

Step 2: Molar mass = mass/moles = 1/0.01 = 100 g/mol

scoreMessages.90: Outstanding! You've mastered mole fractions, molarity from percent, and gas calculations! 🧪📊
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review the relationships between mass percent, density, and molarity.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on using the molar volume at STP (22.4 L/mol) correctly.
scoreMessages.0: Please review mole fraction concepts, molarity calculations, and gas law fundamentals.
